title: sedum subgaleatum
intro: Sedum subgaleatum is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Sedum subgaleatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, such as wounds and eczema. Herbal preparations of Sedum subgaleatum can be made by infusing the dried leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by mixing the plant's extracts with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments.
